records.title: Renting a home: step-by-step plan
records.description: A practical sequence for checking the type and price of a Dutch rental home, reviewing the deposit and service costs, signing a written tenancy agreement and reporting landlord problems.
records.ai_summary: Use the official tenant plan before accepting a home: identify the rental sector, check the maximum rent where applicable, record the property's condition and keep written evidence of every agreement.

records.title: What belongs in a tenancy agreement
records.description: A Dutch tenancy agreement records the parties, the home, rent, payment date, annual rent changes, deposit, service costs and the rules that apply to use and termination of the home.

official_source.title: Welke afspraken staan er in het huurcontract van mijn woning?

records.ai_summary: Read the complete written contract before signing and make sure the basic rent, other charges, duration, deposit and house rules are distinguishable. Keep the signed agreement and the property inspection record.

records.title: When a temporary rental contract is allowed
records.description: Permanent tenancy is the general rule for new rental agreements, while temporary contracts are permitted only for specific groups or legally defined situations and require the correct notice procedure.

official_source.title: Mag mijn verhuurder mij een tijdelijk huurcontract aanbieden?

records.ai_summary: Check whether the tenant and situation fall within an official exception before accepting a temporary contract. The contract label alone does not determine whether a fixed end date is legally valid.

records.title: Check the maximum basic rent
records.description: The Huurcommissie rent check uses the official points system to estimate the maximum permitted basic rent for an independent home, room, caravan or pitch based on its characteristics.
records.ai_summary: Enter the home's features in the official Huurprijscheck and retain the result. The calculated points help determine the applicable rental sector and whether a formal rent assessment may be useful.

records.title: Housing associations and social housing
records.description: Dutch housing associations build, manage and rent homes, with a core duty to provide affordable housing for people with lower incomes and other groups who need suitable accommodation.
records.ai_summary: Housing associations are regulated providers with a public housing role. Access, allocation and registration can vary by region, so applicants should also consult the local housing platform and municipality.

records.title: Service costs and utility charges in rent
records.description: Service costs cover agreed services supplied with a rental home, while gas, water and electricity may be charged separately. The landlord must distinguish these amounts from the basic rent.

official_source.title: Servicekosten en kosten voor gas, water en elektriciteit

records.ai_summary: Check which services and utilities the landlord supplies, how advances are calculated and whether meters are individual or shared. Basic rent and additional costs should remain separately understandable.

records.title: Tenant protection against ending the lease
records.description: Tenant protection limits when a landlord can end a tenancy and normally allows the tenant to object. The exact protection depends on the agreement, the home and the legal ground used.
records.ai_summary: A landlord cannot simply remove a protected tenant. Do not treat a notice as automatically final: verify the stated ground, the contract type, the notice requirements and the route for objection.

records.title: Core rights and duties of a tenant
records.description: Tenants must pay rent on time, use the home responsibly and handle minor repairs, while retaining rights including tenant protection, proper maintenance and information from the landlord.

official_source.title: Welke rechten en plichten heb ik als huurder?

records.ai_summary: Tenancy is a two-sided legal relationship: the tenant pays and behaves responsibly, while the landlord maintains the home and respects statutory protections. Written records help when either side fails to comply.

records.title: Rental agency and mediation fees
records.description: A rental intermediary generally may not charge the prospective tenant mediation fees when acting for the landlord. Other clearly described services must be assessed separately on their facts.

official_source.title: Moet ik bemiddelingskosten betalen voor een kamer of huurwoning?

records.ai_summary: Ask whom the intermediary represents and request an itemised explanation before paying. When the agent works for the landlord, charging the tenant again for mediation is generally not allowed.

records.title: Split an all-in rental price
records.description: An all-in price combines basic rent and other charges in one amount, making them impossible to verify separately. A tenant can ask for the amount to be split into lawful components.
records.ai_summary: If the contract states only one all-in amount, request a split between basic rent and advances for services or utilities. The split enables separate checks of rent increases and annual cost settlements.
